title: Wasteland Quest Hooks
description: Jobs, crises, and entanglements from the broken world — salvage runs, settlement politics, faction disputes, supply emergencies, and the kind of problems that arise when civilization is something people are rebuilding from the parts they can find.

entries.result: A settlement's water purifier has failed and the nearest replacement part is in a pre-collapse facility two days out, in disputed territory.
A supply convoy has gone missing on a route it's run without incident for three years. The settlement that depends on it has ten days of reserves.
A faction is offering salvage rights to a pre-collapse facility in exchange for an escort job they're describing as routine and paying as if it isn't.
A survivor community has found a working pre-collapse agricultural system and needs help getting it operational before the growing season ends.
A map has surfaced showing the location of a pre-collapse seed vault. Two factions already know about it and are moving. A third is paying for someone to get there first.
A child has wandered from a settlement into a radiation zone. She's alive. Getting her out without additional exposure is a technical problem.
A settlement's leader has been taken by a rival faction as a bargaining chip in a dispute that the settlement wasn't aware was happening.

A pre-collapse medical facility has been found mostly intact. The problem is it's inside another faction's territory and the treatment it could provide would change the settlement's survival odds significantly.
A long-range scavenger has come back with coordinates for something large and valuable but is dying of an undiagnosed condition and needs medical help before she can explain what she found.
A faction is blockading a trade route in a dispute with a second faction. The third settlement that depends on that route for food is not party to the dispute and is running out of time.
A pre-collapse communications array has been found working. It's receiving a signal from somewhere further out than anyone has scouted. Someone needs to go find out what's at the other end.
A cult has moved into an abandoned settlement and is recruiting aggressively. Three families from a neighboring community have already joined. Their relatives want them back.
A raider group has been hitting the same settlement on a predictable cycle. The settlement wants to set an ambush but needs people who can be in position without being noticed.
A faction leader's illness has been suppressed — his inner circle has been concealing it — and a succession crisis is developing that two factions are already positioning to exploit.
A pre-collapse military installation has been discovered with its armory intact. Four groups are aware of this. One of them is the party's employer.

A settlement's records have been destroyed — fire, flood, deliberate — and without them, the faction they owe obligations to is claiming non-payment. Proof of the original agreement may exist somewhere.
A long-running trade arrangement between two settlements has collapsed because of a single disputed shipment. Someone needs to find out what actually happened to the goods.
A pre-collapse bunker has been opened and the people inside have emerged, disoriented, with resources and knowledge that are thirty years out of date and immediately valuable.
A survivor community has been quietly disappearing, one family at a time, over the course of months. The remaining families want to know what's happening before they're next.
A faction has taken control of a clean water source and is rationing access in a way that disadvantages the settlements downstream. The downstream settlements need leverage or an alternative.
A scavenger has died in the field leaving behind an encrypted data drive and a community that depends on what's on it.
A new faction is establishing itself in the region, moving quickly, and no one yet knows whether they're a threat, a resource, or something in between.
A pre-collapse research facility contains records of what caused the collapse in this region. Several powerful entities do not want those records found. One is paying to find them.
A settlement's best medic has been offered a position by a faction with more resources and better facilities. The settlement needs to either match the offer or find another solution.

A working vehicle has been found in a pre-collapse garage — functional, fueled, in good condition. Getting it out requires navigating through a gang's territory. The gang doesn't know the vehicle is there.
A faction enforcer has been running a side operation — extortion, on his own — that his faction doesn't know about and that two settlements have been paying into for a year.

A pre-collapse pharmaceutical warehouse has been located. The medications inside are still viable. Getting to them requires passing through an area that was deliberately sealed after the collapse for reasons the seal doesn't explain.
A settlement's engineer has figured out how to restore partial power to an entire district. She needs specific components she can't find and a reason to trust the people who could help her get them.
A refugee group has arrived at a settlement with resources and skills the settlement needs, but also with a faction pursuing them that the settlement can't afford to antagonize.

A pre-collapse satellite has been found to still be operational. Someone with the right knowledge can query it for pre-collapse data. The knowledge is in a specific person who doesn't know they have it.
A faction's leadership has fractured and two successor factions are using the surrounding settlements as proxies in their dispute.
A survivor who claims to know the location of a working desalination facility needs an escort across terrain that killed the last three people who tried the route.

A settlement's crops are failing due to a soil condition that a pre-collapse agricultural extension office would have diagnosed in an hour. The knowledge to diagnose and treat it still exists but is inside a building nobody's gone into.
A faction treaty is about to expire and one side isn't negotiating in good faith. The settlements that benefit from the peace need either an honest negotiation or a contingency plan.

A pre-collapse structure has been discovered with active automated defenses that are preventing access to what's inside. Defeating the defenses requires understanding what they're designed to protect.
A survivor is selling information about a pre-collapse supply cache. Three buyers are interested. The information is genuine. The seller has sold it to all three and has a head start.
A settlement has been given a ninety-day deadline to produce proof of a specific resource claim or the territory reverts to a faction. The proof exists. Finding it is the problem.
A doctor operating independently has been providing medical care to everyone who reaches her, regardless of faction, and has information as a result that multiple factions want and she won't give.

A pre-collapse industrial facility could be restored to partial operation, producing something the region needs badly. It requires specialist knowledge and a security situation that's currently untenable.
A faction is recruiting from a settlement pool in a way that's legal under the regional agreements but is draining the settlement of its most capable people faster than they can be replaced.
A caravan of traders with pre-collapse goods has been moving through the region. They've been welcomed everywhere. One settlement has noticed they leave with more than they arrive with.
A pre-collapse emergency shelter was activated during the collapse and sealed. The people inside may still be alive. The shelter was designed for ten years of self-sufficiency and it's been eighteen.
A faction has acquired a pre-collapse weapon system and is using it to extort neighboring settlements. The system has a specific vulnerability that the faction doesn't know about.
A settlement has developed a medicine that treats the most common post-collapse illness in the region. A faction wants the formula, wants to control production, and is not interested in sharing.
A survivor who has been living alone in the deep wasteland has died, leaving behind a cache that he described in letters to a settlement — letters that arrived after his death.
A pre-collapse government repository of survivor lists — who was documented in the first years after the collapse — has been found. Families are paying to have it searched.

A faction enforcer has gone rogue, moving through the region and taking resources from settlements, using the faction's name. The faction wants her brought in. The settlements want the taking to stop.
A settlement's founding generation is dying. The oral history they carry — critical practical knowledge about local water, soil, seasonal patterns — goes with them unless it's recorded.
A working radio network has been established between three settlements. A fourth settlement wants access. The cost of access is something they don't have and something they might be able to get.
A pre-collapse research program left behind automated systems still running their original mission. What they're doing is becoming a problem.
A faction's supply lines have been hit repeatedly. They believe it's a rival faction. The pattern of hits suggests someone with inside knowledge. The rival faction is also getting hit.
A disease is moving through a region that hasn't seen it before. The nearest person with relevant medical knowledge is in a settlement that currently isn't accepting visitors.
A pre-collapse infrastructure project was never completed. Completing it would benefit everyone in the region. Starting it requires a coalition that doesn't currently trust each other.
A survivor community has been self-sufficient and isolated for twenty years. Something has changed in the last three months and they've started reaching out. Nobody knows why.
A working printing press has been found. Whoever controls it controls information in the region. Three parties are aware of this. The press is currently in neutral hands.

A faction has been using a pre-collapse legal framework to claim resources. Another faction has found a counter-argument in the same framework. A settlement with the relevant records could tip the dispute.
A long-distance trader has been operating a route that passes through six settlements for fifteen years. She has just stopped and no one knows why or where she is.
A settlement child has developed abilities that the settlement doesn't understand and can't explain. Two factions have noticed. The parents are looking for help that doesn't come with strings.
A pre-collapse mining operation left behind tunnels that are now being used by something. The settlement above them wants to know what before they continue building on the surface.
A faction council is about to vote on a policy that will fundamentally change their relationship with neighboring settlements. The vote is three days away. The outcome is one vote uncertain.

A survivor community has reverse-engineered a pre-collapse manufacturing process and is producing a critical trade good. They need distribution. Distribution means exposure to factions they've survived by avoiding.
A pre-collapse environmental monitoring station is detecting something unusual in the regional water supply. The data is public. Nobody is doing anything about it because nobody knows what to do.

A faction has offered a settlement protection in exchange for exclusive use of their agricultural output. The terms are not as favorable as they initially appeared. The contract has already been signed.
A wasteland runner with a large client base has gone silent. Her clients include several settlements and one faction. All of them want to know why, for different reasons.
A pre-collapse automated factory is producing something, at intervals, according to a schedule no one has decoded. The output accumulates at a specific location.
A faction member has defected with information that could reshape the regional balance of power. She needs to be somewhere safe before she talks. She doesn't trust anyone yet.
A settlement has built a defensive wall and the construction has blocked a route used by three other settlements. The wall is finished. Negotiating around it is now the problem.
A pre-collapse weather prediction system has been found functional and is predicting something severe for the region in forty days. Nobody has forty days of preparation resources.
A salvager has found a cache of pre-collapse educational materials — textbooks, technical manuals, scientific literature — and doesn't know what they're worth or to whom.
A faction has been systematically acquiring the debts of small settlements, and several are now so indebted that they can't refuse faction directives without defaulting.
A survivor has mapped an alternate route between two major settlements that avoids all current faction checkpoints. The map is accurate. She wants something for it.
A pre-collapse nuclear facility in the region is showing anomalous readings on instruments that survivors built specifically to monitor it. The readings are new.
A faction has lost control of one of its outposts. The outpost has gone quiet but hasn't been taken — the faction's personnel are still there but not responding to contact.

A working pre-collapse vehicle repair manual has been found. The manual covers a vehicle type that several settlements have but can't maintain. Getting the manual is straightforward. Keeping it is not.
A settlement council has been infiltrated by a faction plant who has been subtly redirecting community decisions for two years. Someone has figured it out. Nobody knows what to do about it.
A pre-collapse satellite phone system has been found partially functional. It can reach one specific location. Nobody knows what's at that location.

A faction is claiming a section of territory that three settlement families have worked for a generation. The faction has documentation. So do the families. Neither set of documents covers the same events.
A settlement's best engineer is being coerced into working for a faction. The coercion is non-violent and technically legal. The settlement wants it stopped.
A pre-collapse fuel depot has been found with viable reserves. The faction that finds it first controls mobility in the region for the next three years.

A series of coordinated raids has hit every settlement in a valley on the same night, taking a specific category of goods from each. Nobody has figured out what was taken collectively, only individually.

A survivor with a specific pre-collapse technical skill is the only person in the region who can maintain a critical system. She's dying. She's willing to teach someone. The faction that controls her won't let her.
A pre-collapse military installation's AI systems are still running, still following their last orders, and their last orders have become relevant again.
A settlement has been operating a protection scheme on smaller communities in their vicinity, framing it as mutual defense. One community has decided to stop paying.

A long-term survivor project — restoring a specific piece of pre-collapse infrastructure — is thirty years in and close to completion. The faction that benefits most has decided to take it over at the finish line.
A child born after the collapse has abilities that appear to be a direct result of post-collapse environmental conditions. A faction's research division wants to study her. Her parents have fled.
A working aquifer has been identified under wasteland territory. Accessing it requires drilling equipment the region doesn't have and cooperation between factions that currently don't have it.
A faction's internal purge has produced a wave of refugees with valuable skills and dangerous information. The faction is hunting them. The settlements are deciding whether to shelter them.
A pre-collapse communication network has been found with records of the first years after the collapse — who was where, what resources existed, what happened to them.
A new food blight is moving through the region's crops. The cause is unknown. The only person who might recognize it is in a settlement that's three days away and not currently accessible.
A faction's long-range patrol has found something in the deep wasteland they haven't reported through official channels. Three of the six patrol members have since left the faction.
A settlement has been functioning well for fifteen years. In the last three months it has been making increasingly bad decisions. Nobody inside it has noticed.

A pre-collapse supply cache left by a disaster preparedness organization contains equipment for rebuilding specific types of infrastructure. The cache is in a location that will require a small expedition.
A faction negotiator has been killed en route to a peace summit. Both sides believe the other did it. A third party has reason to want the summit to fail.
A working regional map database has been found in a pre-collapse planning office. It shows every infrastructure installation in the region as of thirty years ago. Most are destroyed. Not all.
A survivor community of former pre-collapse technical specialists has been hidden in plain sight for two decades. They've finally decided to engage with the world. They have conditions.
A faction has been poisoning a trade route — subtly, gradually — to redirect commerce through their controlled territory. The evidence is there for someone who knows where to look.

A pre-collapse gene bank has been found with viable agricultural strains that could transform food production in the region. Getting it out requires solving a containment system designed to keep it in.

A faction has built a weapon they don't fully understand, using a pre-collapse design they don't have complete documentation for. They've tested it once. The test crater is visible from forty kilometers.

A survivor network that has been quietly connecting communities across the region for twenty years has decided to come out of the shadows. Their first act is making contact with someone they believe can be trusted.

A pre-collapse bunker sealed at the beginning of the collapse has opened from the inside. The people who emerge are healthy, educated, well-supplied, and have a plan for the region. Nobody asked them to.
